---
title: "Template Title"
author: "Your Name"
date: "The Date"
output: output_format
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Let's explore the 'one-sample t test'


```{r}
library(ggt.test)
library(tidyverse)
```


# Scenario 1: On average are is peoples' estimate of song-snippet length correct? The true length of the snippet played is 10 seconds 

What is the sample size?

>

What is the mean estimate for the sample?

> 

What is the null?  

>

Does the mean for the sample look consistent with the null?


```{r}



```


# Scenario 2: Do non-cadet undergraduate students have a mean of grit scores that are consistent West Point's mean of 3.75

What is the 'n'

>

What is the sample mean?

>

What is the asserted mean (null) $\mu = ?$

>

Does the sample mean for the students, look like the asserted mean (West Point cadets), i.e. can I reject the null hypothesis?

>


# Scenario 3: Hours of sleep.

Students are asked to report their hours of sleep from the night previous.  Is there evidence that on average, there sleep differs from the recommended 8 hours? 

How many students are in the sample?

> 22

What is the mean number of hours? (balancing point)

>

What is the null hypothesis? (put it on the plot) 

> 8

Does it look like our campus population on average get 8 hours of sleep, or is what we observe inconsistn with this proposed mean, $\mu = 8$ 

> looks inconsistent with the proposed 8 hrs average.

```{r}
data_hrs_sleep
```
